commands.author: mushuiyu886

target.title: fix(ui): localize Control UI docs links
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: localized Control UI users could open built-in docs links and land on default English docs routes.
- Solution: centralize docs href construction in `buildDocsHref()` and render matching localized docs routes for supported docs locales.
- What changed: sidebar docs, overview docs hints, login gate docs hints, missing-scope attention docs, and agent channel setup docs now use locale-aware docs hrefs.
- What was not changed: English/default docs behavior, unsupported-locale fallback behavior, generated locale bundles, localized docs content, auth/session/gateway behavior.
- Related: #88570

## What Problem This Solves

The Control UI can render non-English labels, but fixed built-in documentation anchors were still rendered as default English `https://docs.openclaw.ai/...` hrefs. That sends a user from a localized UI state to English docs even when the same docs route has a localized docs prefix available.

This fixes the mismatch at the href-rendering source: the UI now builds docs links from the active UI locale and a known localized-docs allowlist, instead of hardcoding default docs URLs at each rendered link.

## User Impact

- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users who switch the Control UI to Simplified Chinese or another supported localized UI locale now keep that language context when opening built-in Control UI docs links. English users and users with unsupported locale values keep the previous default English docs URLs, so the change improves localized navigation without changing the default path.

## Origin / follow-up

- **Follows / completes:** #88570 follow-up.
- **Gap this fills:** localization covered Control UI text, but same-class built-in docs anchors still pointed at default English docs routes.
- **Consistency:** this follows the locale-registry/docs-publish mapping by applying locale-aware docs routing at render time rather than editing generated locale bundles or localized docs files.

## Competition / linked PR analysis

- **Linked PR(s):** None found for this exact Control UI docs-link locale mismatch.
- **Gap in linked PR(s):** N/A — no linked open PR was found that changes these fixed Control UI docs hrefs.
- **Why this patch is better/canonical:** the patch changes the docs-link construction helper used by UI render sites, instead of adding one-off downstream string patches per locale.
- **Local real behavior proof advantage:** the proof clicks the rendered zh-CN sidebar docs link in a running Control UI and records the opened docs URL.
- **Related open PR scan:** no related open PR for the same docs-link locale contract was identified before preparing this follow-up.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Built-in Control UI docs links should open the localized docs route when the UI is set to a supported localized locale.
- **Real environment tested:** Mock Control UI launched from this branch at `http://127.0.0.1:5197/overview`, driven in Google Chrome via Playwright.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
1. Open http://127.0.0.1:5197/overview.
2. Change the Control UI language selector to Simplified Chinese.
3. Confirm the rendered UI includes the Chinese docs label 文档.
4. Click the left sidebar 文档 link.
5. Observe the newly opened docs tab URL.
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-followup-88570-evidence/control-ui-zh-cn-docs-link.json
/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-followup-88570-evidence/control-ui-zh-cn-docs-link.png
```

- **Observed result after fix:**

```text
footerText: 文档
footerHref: https://docs.openclaw.ai/zh-CN/
popupUrl: https://docs.openclaw.ai/zh-CN
passed: true
```

- **What was not tested:** Did not browser-click every supported locale. The helper regression test covers URL construction for every current non-English UI locale; the browser proof exercises the real rendered/clicked UI path for zh-CN.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Review findings addressed

N/A — new follow-up PR, no review findings yet.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `ui/src/ui/external-link.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/views/overview.render.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/views/login-gate.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/views/agents.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** zh-CN rendered docs affordances now point to `https://docs.openclaw.ai/zh-CN/...` instead of default English docs URLs; English keeps default docs URLs; unsupported locale values fall back to default docs URLs; absolute docs URLs with anchors preserve the anchor while adding the locale prefix.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** one helper test locks the complete locale mapping and fallback contract, while focused render tests lock each fixed built-in docs-link surface that previously hardcoded a default docs href.

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/external-link.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/overview.render.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/login-gate.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/agents.test.ts

Test Files  6 passed (6)
Tests       90 passed (90)
```

## Risk / Compatibility

- Contract surface: user-visible external docs hrefs rendered by the Control UI.
- Canonical source-of-truth: Control UI supported locales come from the UI i18n registry; localized docs support is represented in the helper allowlist that matches the generated docs locales.
- Compatibility/default behavior: English remains `https://docs.openclaw.ai/...`; unsupported locale values use the same default URL rather than constructing a missing localized route.
- Out of scope: no config, schema, protocol, migration, auth credential validation, session state, gateway connection, or docs-content behavior is changed.
- Related open PR scan: no related open PR for this same docs-link locale contract was found before this follow-up.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** compatibility, auth-provider, availability, session-state.
- **Risk explanation:** merge risk is from touched UI files that contain auth/login, availability, and session-link wording, but this patch only changes rendered documentation href construction. It does not change auth decisions, token/password handling, session key state, gateway availability checks, websocket connection behavior, or error classification.
- **Why acceptable:** the production change is a centralized docs-link mapping plus render-site href replacement, backed by per-surface render regression tests and a real browser click proof. The larger file count comes from testing every built-in docs-link surface touched by the same root cause, not from broad behavior changes.

## What was not changed

- **What did NOT change:** no public API, schema, protocol, default gateway behavior, auth/session behavior, provider/channel behavior, generated non-English UI bundles, localized docs content, lockfiles, formatting-only cleanup, or unrelated refactor was changed.
- Public API / schema / protocol / defaults: unchanged.
- Channel/provider/session/security behavior outside the fixed path: unchanged.
- Unrelated cleanup, docs, formatting, lockfiles: unchanged.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** Fixed Control UI docs anchors were hardcoded to default `https://docs.openclaw.ai/...` URLs at each render site, causing the href output source to ignore the active UI locale mapping even when localized docs routes existed for that same route.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** `buildDocsHref()` moves the locale decision to the docs-link construction source before the anchor is rendered, then each built-in docs-link render site consumes that helper. The source mapping now emits localized hrefs for supported localized docs locales and emits the existing default href for English or unsupported locale values, so the incorrect href is not produced.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High — the change is limited to docs-link href construction, every fixed render surface has regression coverage, and the rendered/clicked zh-CN sidebar docs path was verified in a browser.
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch quality warnings are expected false positives from files that contain auth-provider, availability, and session-state UI context. This patch does not mask symptoms with downstream fallback logic; it changes the source docs href mapping and keeps fallback only for unsupported docs locales to preserve existing default behavior.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth boundary is the UI locale registry plus the docs localized-locale allowlist that matches generated docs locales. The helper is the canonical docs href construction point for fixed Control UI chrome links; generated locale bundles and localized docs content remain outside this PR.
